(auto) ensure agent ends day at home

diary: Woke up at 6:15, coffee first, then a quick tidy and letting the dog stretch his legs at Juanita Bay. The morning felt bright in that early-June way—like the whole day was already leaning toward “good.” After breakfast I checked the shop schedule and parts list, and headed out toward Woodinville. By the time I parked at the independent garage, I was already thinking through an engine rattle and a brake that wasn’t behaving right. I spent the late morning troubleshooting, calling the parts supplier, and trying not to lose my patience (mostly succeeded). Lunch was Taco Time, eaten outside because the sun was too nice to waste. Later, I grabbed small supplies at Ace Hardware, then finished the day with test drives and inspections. The World Cup match had bars downtown opening early, so I drove in to catch USA vs Wales at Pike Place—fun crowd, good energy. Back home, groceries unloaded, dog walk done, and I finally crashed feeling lighter than I expected.
thought_process: I started the day with the usual rhythm—coffee, tidy, schedule check—because if I don’t get my head straight early, the garage day starts piling up faster than I can sort it. I chose to eat lunch outside because the weather was sunny and mild; it gave me a mental reset right when troubleshooting can get repetitive. The light rail closure didn’t hit me directly since I drive, but it still made me think about downtown traffic and timing, so I left for the bar in good time. World events mattered more than I expected: knowing USA vs Wales was on at 11am PDT made the afternoon feel slightly energized, like everyone was gearing up. My mood shifted from focused and tense during repairs to genuinely relaxed once I sat down for the match, then quiet content at home.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
